mechanism_narrative: A persistently high UNRATE indicates slack in the labor market, depressing consumer spending and corporate earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors adjust expectations downward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of UNRATE vs 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or the correlation is consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls to or below 0.25%, it signals a committed accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and supports asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the next 20-day SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate synchronized demand and supply recovery, reinforcing each other and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as economic momentum solidifies.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: When both proxies rise, the 20-day forward return distribution is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) in a low-inflation environment signals genuine economic expansion without overheating, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where trailing 12-month CPI is below 2%, the correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) signals broad economic expansion, driving corporate earnings growth and investor optimism, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 60 trading days (approximately one quarter).
failure_condition: The quarterly change in GDPC1 has a Rank IC below 0.05 for 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window, or the sign of correlation is negative in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,000 thousand units (annualized), it indicates robust housing market activity that boosts related industries and consumer wealth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1000, the next 5-day SPX return is negative more than 55% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals genuine labor market tightening, which raises aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls month-over-month
failure_condition: In months when both conditions hold, the 20-day forward return distribution has a mean below zero or the win rate is less than 50%, contradicting the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1%) and low trailing inflation (CPIAUCSL 12-month change < 2%) indicates a policy environment that is accommodative without immediate inflationary threat, encouraging risk-taking and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the next 20-day SPX return is negative more than 45% of the time, or the Sharpe ratio of such periods is below 0.5 annualized.
